text: Stress is the body's response to perceived threats. Acute stress (fight-or-flight) is adaptive; chronic stress damages health (cardiovascular, immune, mental). Management: exercise, mindfulness/meditation, social connection, time in nature, deep breathing, therapy (CBT). Zebras don't get ulcers because they turn stress responses off.
Breathing: 4-7-8 technique (inhale 4s, hold 7s, exhale 8s) activates parasympathetic nervous system.
Mindfulness: non-judgmental present-moment awareness — 8 weeks MBSR reduces anxiety by ~40%.
